mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This signal is based on the 3-month change in payrolls.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market is a cornerstone of economic expansions.
failure_condition: The signal's rank information coefficient (IC) is negative over a 3-year rolling window, indicating that periods of strong job growth consistently precede negative S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS), defined as a month-over-month decline exceeding 1.0%, signals an abrupt weakening of consumer demand. This negatively impacts revenue forecasts for consumer-facing companies, leading to lower equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes a majority of US GDP.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days, suggesting the market ignores or looks through consumer weakness.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This combination increases the risk of a future, more aggressive tightening cycle, raising discount rates and creating uncertainty, which is negative for equities.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when this condition is met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of 0.5 percentage points or more from its 12-month low is a reliable real-time indicator of a recession's onset (Sahm Rule). This structural shift in the labor market leads to a sharp repricing of recession risk in equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp from its 12-month rolling minimum
failure_condition: The S&P 500 fails to have a negative median 20-day return in the three months following a trigger event across multiple historical recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This is a leading indicator for corporate capital expenditures and earnings, supporting higher equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a key component of the Coincident Economic Activity Index.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10) over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals falling confidence and investment in a key interest-rate sensitive sector. This has negative spillover effects on broader economic activity and consumer wealth, leading to negative short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a major leading indicator of the business cycle.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a trigger event is non-negative over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously slowing real GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) is a powerful confirmation of a broad-based economic downturn. This joint signal indicates both output and labor markets are deteriorating, leading to a severe downgrade in earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth < 1.0% (annualized) and UNRATE increased in the last 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:NBER recession dating considers both output and employment.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, indicating the market is forward-looking and prices in the downturn before the signal occurs.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signifies restrictive monetary policy, which increases the cost of capital for firms and slows economic activity by design. This compression of valuation multiples and earnings growth weighs on equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy aims to influence aggregate demand via the interest rate channel.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return when FEDFUNDS is above 3.0% is not statistically lower than when it is below 3.0% (t-test p > 0.05).
